surface.name: Razorpay Payments product selection and first test transaction
surface.selection_basis: Razorpay's general Quickstart explicitly requires the developer to select a product based on business needs. It presents no-code products and multiple SDK, API, plugin, and payment-gateway integrations without selecting one universal route. A complete Web Standard Checkout candidate is preserved because Razorpay recommends Standard over Quick when the developer wants more control, but that recommendation is conditional and does not resolve the general product choice.

alternatives_considered.name: Payment Links
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Quickstart presents no-code products as a business-needs choice, not as the universal developer first route.

alternatives_considered.name: Quick Integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Razorpay calls this integration simple and fast, but recommends Standard when more control over integration and checkout is needed.

alternatives_considered.name: Ecommerce plugins
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These require an existing supported commerce platform and each has a separate configuration path.

alternatives_considered.name: Mobile and server integrations
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are peer surfaces chosen according to the application's platform and architecture.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Razorpay account, choose the product and integration matching the business, and complete its first simulated Test Mode transaction.
assumed_prior_state: No Razorpay account, merchant ID, KYC state, website, API key, product choice, application, order, or payment is assumed.
The representative candidate assumes an India account, an unregistered individual business, a web application, and permission to act as account Owner.
KYC completion is not assumed for the test candidate because Razorpay says API or SDK integrations can start in Test Mode during KYC.
No account creation, OTP, KYC, key generation, order creation, or test transaction was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: A successful Test Mode payment for the selected product or integration
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer completes one simulated Razorpay transaction using the product route selected for the business.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: For the preserved Web Standard Checkout candidate, Checkout returns razorpay_payment_id, razorpay_order_id, and razorpay_signature; server verification succeeds; and Dashboard shows the payment as Captured.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Razorpay's integration guides instruct the developer to make a test payment and define a successful payment as Captured in Dashboard.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The general Quickstart says to choose a product and test before going live. The exact implementation and signal vary by that choice, so the success class is documented while a universal chronological route is not.

prerequisites.requirement: A Razorpay account with Dashboard access.
Verified email or phone contact plus business and onboarding details required for the chosen account type.
Owner or Admin access to generate API keys for an API, SDK, or plugin integration.
Test Mode Key ID and Key Secret for routes that use Razorpay APIs or SDKs.
A product and integration choice based on business requirements and application platform.

primary_path.output: The account, product-selection, Dashboard-action, testing, and go-live framework opens.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide identifies account creation as Step 1 and product selection as Step 2.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No documentation navigation recovery is specified.

primary_path.action: Open Razorpay signup, enter an email address or 10-digit phone number, create a valid password, enter the delivered OTP, click Verify, click Submit OTP, select a business type, and click Proceed.
primary_path.input: Contact, password, OTP, and business type
primary_path.success_signal: Razorpay advances to full name and mobile details.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use Resend OTP if the initial OTP does not arrive.

details: Enter full name and continue.
Enter mobile number and continue.
Enter the mobile OTP and continue.
Select platforms for accepting payments and continue.
Add website or app links, or select Add later.
Add social links, or select Add later.
Review the details and continue.
primary_path.input: Identity, mobile, platform, and optional web or social links
primary_path.success_signal: Business type and subtype controls are shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Platform, business, and region choices change later actions.

primary_path.action: Select registered or unregistered business, choose the business subtype, and complete the matching business-detail branch.
details: An unregistered individual provides personal PAN, confirms the fetched PAN details, selects or enters a brand name, reviews, and continues.
Registered-business onboarding additionally requires business identity and documents.
primary_path.output: Razorpay records the account's business details and opens the matching KYC flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The account proceeds into CKYC or traditional KYC.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Required PAN and document details depend on the business type.

primary_path.action: Complete the applicable CKYC or traditional KYC submission path, add and verify bank details, submit the application, and choose Explore Test Dashboard.
details: An unregistered individual first attempts CKYC with the linked mobile number and OTP.
If CKYC fails, Razorpay documents DigiLocker and manual identity-proof routes, and may require Video KYC.

The onboarding path also collects business category and sub-category, conditional delivery-time information, and bank details; Razorpay deposits 1 rupee for bank verification and requests proof if verification fails.
primary_path.input: Branch-specific identity, KYC, business, and bank information
primary_path.output: The application is submitted for review and the Test Dashboard entry is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The page offers Explore Test Dashboard while Live Mode remains pending verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: CKYC failure adds identity-proof and potentially Video KYC actions; Razorpay may request clarification during review.

primary_path.action: Open Dashboard in Test Mode while KYC verification is pending or completed.
details: Test Mode uses dummy transactions, moves no real money, and can be used during KYC verification.
primary_path.output: Razorpay products are available for testing.
primary_path.success_signal: The Dashboard exposes Test Mode and product configuration.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Live Mode remains gated on KYC verification and activation.

primary_path.action: Select the Razorpay product and integration method that matches business needs and technical capabilities.
details: The Quickstart does not select no-code, plugin, SDK, API, web, mobile, or server route for the developer.
primary_path.output: A product-specific integration guide is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: No single official universal next step exists at this decision.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The chronological path and terminal fields change materially with the product.

candidate_paths.name: India unregistered-individual account to Web Standard Checkout test payment
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This is one complete official candidate for a web developer who wants checkout control. Razorpay recommends Standard over Quick when more control is wanted, but the candidate does not claim to be universal.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates the account-type and product choices left open in primary steps 4 and 7, preserves the documented KYC submission branches before Test Dashboard access, then continues through test keys, order creation, Checkout, signature verification, and Captured status.

steps.action: Open Razorpay's Payments Quickstart and follow the signup link.
Enter email or a 10-digit phone number, create a password, enter the delivered OTP, click Verify, click Submit OTP, choose unregistered individual business, and click Proceed.
steps.input: Contact, password, OTP, and business classification

steps.action: Enter full name, mobile number, and mobile OTP, selecting Continue after each action.
Select website as the platform, continue, add the website and social links or defer them, review Basic Details, and continue.
steps.failure_or_wait: Links can be added later for the documented signup path.

steps.action: Select unregistered and the individual subtype, enter personal PAN, confirm the fetched PAN details, select or enter the brand name, review the business details, and continue.
steps.success_signal: Razorpay records the individual-business details and requests CKYC or traditional KYC information.
steps.failure_or_wait: Incorrect PAN details must be edited before confirmation.

steps.action: Complete the available unregistered-individual KYC submission path, add bank details, submit the application, and click Explore Test Dashboard.
details: Enter the mobile number linked to CKYC, generate and verify the OTP, and use the fetched details if CKYC succeeds.
If CKYC fails, follow Razorpay's DigiLocker or manual identity-proof path; Video KYC may also be required.
Select and confirm the business category and sub-category, provide average delivery time when requested, and add bank details.
Razorpay deposits 1 rupee to verify the bank account; if verification fails, upload the documented bank-account proof.
Click Submit Application, then Explore Test Dashboard while verification remains pending.
steps.input: CKYC or identity proof, business classification, delivery information when requested, and bank details
steps.output: The application is submitted and Test Dashboard access is offered.
steps.success_signal: Explore Test Dashboard is available before Live Mode activation.
steps.failure_or_wait: CKYC failure adds identity-proof and potentially Video KYC actions; Razorpay may request clarification during review.

steps.action: In Test Mode, open Account & Settings, open API Keys under Website and app settings, and click Generate Key.
details: Only Owner and Admin roles can access API keys.
Razorpay says Test Mode keys can be generated without adding a website.
steps.output: A Test Mode Key ID and Key Secret are generated.
steps.failure_or_wait: A non-Owner or non-Admin role cannot generate keys.

steps.action: Download and securely save the Key ID and Key Secret.
details: The secret is visible only when generated.
steps.output: The application can authenticate test API requests.
steps.failure_or_wait: A lost secret requires key regeneration and application updates.

steps.action: Create an order on the server with a Test Mode authenticated POST to `/v1/orders`.
details: Send amount and currency; receipt, notes, and partial-payment fields are optional.
steps.input: Key ID, Key Secret, amount, and currency
steps.output: Razorpay returns an order entity containing an order id.
steps.success_signal: The server retains the returned order id for Checkout.
steps.failure_or_wait: Payments without an order_id cannot be captured and are automatically refunded.

steps.action: Add a Pay button and load `https://checkout.razorpay.com/v1/checkout.js` on the web page.
steps.success_signal: The page displays the Pay button.
steps.failure_or_wait: The Checkout script must load before the button handler opens it.

steps.action: Create Razorpay Checkout options with the Test Key ID, amount, currency, business name, description, returned order_id, handler or callback, optional prefill, notes, and theme.
steps.output: A Razorpay Checkout object is initialized.
steps.success_signal: The button click handler can call `open()`.
steps.failure_or_wait: The Checkout order_id must match the server-created order.

steps.action: Click the Pay button and complete a simulated payment using Razorpay's Test Mode payment data.
details: The mock payment page exposes Success and Failure controls and moves no real money.
steps.success_signal: The success handler or callback runs.
steps.failure_or_wait: Selecting Failure produces the documented failure route rather than success.

steps.action: Return razorpay_payment_id, razorpay_order_id, and razorpay_signature to the configured success handler or callback.
steps.output: The application receives the three success fields.
steps.failure_or_wait: Only successful authorizations are submitted by the documented success route.

steps.action: Verify the payment signature on the server using the order id, payment id, and Key Secret.
details: Generate the expected signature from `order_id|razorpay_payment_id` with HMAC SHA256 and compare it with razorpay_signature.
steps.input: Order id, payment id, returned signature, and Key Secret
steps.output: The application determines whether the payment response is authentic.
steps.success_signal: The generated and returned signatures match.
steps.failure_or_wait: A mismatch must be treated as an unauthenticated payment response.

steps.action: Open Dashboard, navigate to Transactions and Payments, and locate the generated payment id.
steps.output: The Test Mode payment record is displayed.
steps.failure_or_wait: If the payment is not captured, inspect the payment state and capture configuration before treating it as success.

branches.path: Provide business identity, business PAN and incorporation information, plus the required and additional business documents before continuing KYC.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds document and business-verification actions before account activation, while Test Mode may be used during KYC.

branches.path: Use DigiLocker or manual identity proof and complete Video KYC if Razorpay requires it before onboarding submission.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds identity sharing, document, device-permission, and potentially agent-assisted verification actions before Test Dashboard access.

branches.path: Follow the selected Payment Links, Payment Pages, Payment Button, Invoice, QR, or Webstore guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes or changes API-key, order, SDK, and signature steps.

branches.path: Use the default Pay with Razorpay button and form-data callback route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Uses the simple and fast but less controllable Checkout setup.

branches.path: Select the matching SDK, server integration, or plugin documentation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes installation, credential placement, execution interface, and confirmation signal.

friction_gates.description: Razorpay requires an account with verified contact information.
Signup requires OTP verification for the initial contact and mobile number.
Business type and subtype determine PAN, document, CKYC, and KYC actions.
The documented India setup path requires KYC submission and bank verification before it offers Explore Test Dashboard; Test Mode integration can then proceed while KYC verification is in progress.
The general Quickstart requires the developer to choose among materially different products and integration methods.

sources.title: Set up a Razorpay Account
evidence_supported: Test Mode availability after account creation

uncertainties.question: Which Razorpay product and integration should represent the platform's universal first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The code, credentials, test interface, and terminal fields differ materially.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official general Quickstart instructs developers to choose based on business requirements and does not rank one universal route.

uncertainties.question: Which regional and business-type onboarding branch should represent all developers?
uncertainties.impact: Identity, PAN, documents, CKYC, and KYC actions vary.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official setup guide branches by registered status, business subtype, and verification availability.

excluded_after_success: Completing KYC, generating Live Mode keys, whitelisting production sites, and accepting real payments occur after the first simulated payment.
Webhooks, refunds, settlements, disputes, subscriptions, additional payment-method approvals, and production capture policy are outside the first-success boundary.
